─[ Claude CLI Rejects --dangerously-skip-permissions as Root ]─ ┐

name: claude-cli-no-root-skip-perms description: Claude Code CLI rejects —dangerously-skip-permissions when running as root/sudo user-invocable: false allowed-tools: Bash, Read

Claude CLI Rejects —dangerously-skip-permissions as Root

When running Claude Code CLI in containers or automated environments, --dangerously-skip-permissions is required for non-interactive use. The CLI refuses this flag when the process runs as root (UID 0) or under sudo.

Snags

The CLI performs a privilege check before accepting --dangerously-skip-permissions. If the effective UID is 0 (root) or the process was launched via sudo, it exits immediately with:

--dangerously-skip-permissions cannot be used with root/sudo privileges for security reasons

No output is written to stdout. The error goes to stderr. If stderr is redirected and the exit code is masked (e.g., || true), the failure is silent — the job appears to succeed with empty output.

This affects any container image where the default user is root, which includes most base images (node, python, ubuntu, alpine, debian).

Workaround

Run the Claude CLI process as a non-root user. Three approaches depending on environment:

Kubernetes (securityContext):

spec:
  securityContext:
    runAsUser: 1000
    runAsGroup: 1000
    fsGroup: 1000
  containers:
    - env:
        - name: HOME
          value: /home/node  # must be writable by the UID

Dockerfile (USER directive):

FROM node:18-slim
# node user (UID 1000) already exists in node images
USER node

Runtime (su-exec / gosu):

exec su-exec node claude -p "$PROMPT" --dangerously-skip-permissions

In all cases, ensure $HOME points to a writable directory — Claude CLI creates $HOME/.claude/ for session data and project metadata.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
